author: @bobandrews705
text: Eureka!       This works better than 1702.        Great videos!         Watched it four times but got the specifics.

author: @sigridkoelle1932
text: Mason Jar Sock = stainless steel filter for making cold brew coffee or tea in a mason jar.  One guy said he used it as a fuel filter for his tractor.  👍

author: @divingsnow
text: Love your video Rob. Ré video 1702, I made the methanol heater using 5mm carbon felt but can't get the long burn time. Presently only getting half an hour. Thought perhaps the mantel I made is acting too much as a chimney and drawing out the fuel too quick. Love the design. Any advice to bring burn time up to 4 hours?

replies.author: @mike289homebuilt5
replies.text: Steve I also made video 1702.    I was worried about flame height So,   I found 2 small cans that are tight fitting 2 millimeters and used two layers of sock for a wick.    Have to use a spray bottle to spray wet wick twice.  I get 4 hour burn time.  My inner can is open at bottom so it holds about 8 to 12 ounces

replies.author: @dorine888
replies.text: @mike289homebuilt5 awesome :) what;s in the spray bottle? what's the diameter of the cans? thanks!

author: @salaciouscreations4323
text: I am getting no where near the burn time this guy is.    He claims 5 hours.  1ltr of methanol is giving me 45 min.

author: @veronicarobinson9916
text: I need the cardon felt when looking on Amazon it doesn't say 100% cardon felt so can you tell me where to purchase it please.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you can just use a bandage, sock, cotton or even paper

replies.author: @NulodPBall
replies.text:
Or look for carbon fiber felt, or carbon fiber blanket - try looking until you you find one 6mm thick (1/4").

But an old sock or paper towel works, you just have to replace them more often.

replies.author: @sophiepoint6270
replies.text: Flame protector in the welding section of your tool store

replies.author: @NulodPBall
replies.text: It's pretty useless if it's not 100% carbon fiber... if you're looking in the welding or plumbing section.

replies.author: @billmeade9029
replies.text: Look for high temperature carbon fiber welders protective blanket, it's the same thing I just bought some on Amazon, hope this helps 👍

author: @pattiannepascual
text: what is that thing on top of the cans?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: a mason jar coffee filter or strainer it's stainless steel mesh

replies.author: @jamaraquai4224
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering You've possibly said this in your videos before, but any time you're heating metal, we would recommend clarifying that the steel you're using is stainless and/or plain steel and *not* galvanized since heating galvanized steel is incredibly dangerous/toxic and should only happen in well-ventilated areas. Food for thought! Can never be too safe. ;)
